name: Spygate Full
description: Spygate is a conspiracy theory alleging that high-ranking officials within the United States intelligence community and the Democratic Party illegally spied on Donald Trump's presidential campaign during the 2016 election to undermine his chances of winning and, later, to sabotage his presidency. Proponents claim this involved illegal surveillance, unmasking of identities, and leaks to the media. The theory often implicates figures like Barack Obama, Hillary Clinton, James Comey, and John Brennan. Evidence cited by proponents includes the Steele dossier, FISA warrants obtained to surveil Carter Page, and the actions of individuals like Joseph Mifsud. Mainstream perspectives largely dismiss Spygate as a baseless conspiracy theory, arguing that the FBI's investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 election was justified based on credible evidence of Russian attempts to influence the election. They contend that the FISA warrants were legally obtained and that the investigation was conducted within proper channels. Critics also point out inconsistencies and factual errors within the narratives promoted by Spygate proponents and emphasize the lack of evidence supporting claims of widespread illegal spying motivated by political bias. The term 'Spygate' itself is considered politically charged and intended to delegitimize the Russia investigation.
subtitle: Alleged illegal spying on Trump campaign.

claims.claim: Obama administration illegally spied on Trump campaign.
claims.evidence: Unmasking requests, FISA warrants on Carter Page.
claims.criticism: Unmasking was routine; FISA warrants were legally obtained based on probable cause of foreign intelligence activities.

claims.claim: The Steele dossier was used to initiate the Russia investigation.
claims.evidence: Dossier's contents and dissemination to media.
claims.criticism: The FBI's investigation predated the Steele Dossier. While the dossier was used for a FISA warrant, the application also included other evidence.

claims.claim: The FBI was politically motivated to target Trump.
claims.evidence: Statements and actions of Comey, Strzok, and Page.
claims.criticism: Inspector General reports found no evidence of political bias influencing the investigation's initiation, although some individual actions were criticized.

keyFigures.name: Sean Hannity
keyFigures.role: Media advocate of the Spygate theory

keyFigures.name: Andrew McCabe
keyFigures.role: Deputy Director of the FBI during the Russia investigation, often targeted by the theory

keyFigures.name: Peter Strzok
keyFigures.role: FBI agent involved in the Russia investigation, often targeted by the theory

timeline.event: FBI begins investigation into Russian interference.
Release of Nunes memo alleging FISA abuse.
Barr appoints John Durham to investigate the origins of the Russia investigation.

sources.author: Sidney Powell
sources.work: Licensed to Lie: Exposing Corruption in the Department of Justice
